legacy.blocked: Nevela's Automation Suite is incompatible with the legacy module(s) {legacyList}. These modules are no longer supported. Disable them to use Nevela's Automation Suite. (The legacy migration tool remains available.)

enableDamageAutomation.name: Enable Damage Automation
enableDamageAutomation.hint: When disabled, Nevela's Automation Suite will not modify PF1 damage application behavior (it will pass through to the system).

enableMetamagicAutomation.name: Enable Metamagic Automation
enableMetamagicAutomation.hint: When disabled, Nevela's Automation Suite will not add metamagic UI or automation (e.g. persistent/dazing save workflow, empower tooltip overrides).

enforceSpellAbilityMinimum.name: Enforce Spell Ability Minimum
enforceSpellAbilityMinimum.hint: Enforce PF1 spellcasting minimum ability score requirements (10 + spell level). When enabled, spells above the caster's ability limit are blocked for use and assignment, while still honoring spellbooks marked with no ability limit.

damageTypePriority.name: Custom Damage Type Priority
damageTypePriority.hint: Add custom damage types to be counted as damage reduction types. Magic is at least 1, Cold Iron and Silver types are 3, Adamantine is 4 and Alignments are 5 by default.

damageTypePriorityMenu.name: Configure Damage Type Priority
damageTypePriorityMenu.hint: Open the configuration form to set damage type priority.

customSetting.label: Configure Custom Damage Types
customSetting.hint: Add or edit custom damage types for the module.

massiveDamage.name: Massive Damage Rule
massiveDamage.hint: Enable the optional Massive Damage rule (Pathfinder Core Rulebook p.189). When a creature takes damage from a single attack equal to or greater than its massive damage threshold, it must make a Fortitude save or die.

metamagicCastTimeRule.name: Metamagic Cast Time
metamagicCastTimeRule.hint: Choose how metamagic affects spontaneous casting time.

persistentSpellTargetMode.name: Persistent Spell Targets
persistentSpellTargetMode.hint: Choose how targets are determined when using the chat save button for Persistent Spell.

choices.message: Use targets stored on the chat card

metamagicChatCardNameMode.name: Metamagic Chat Card Name
metamagicChatCardNameMode.hint: Control whether metamagic adds a prefix to the chat card item name.

choices.off: Do not change chat card name

metamagicPreviewMode.name: Metamagic Preview Display
metamagicPreviewMode.hint: Choose how much metamagic preview information appears in the spell dialog.

saveRollTokenInteraction.name: Save Roll Token Interaction
saveRollTokenInteraction.hint: Add a clickable token image to saving throw roll messages.

enhancedDiceTooltipMode.name: Enhanced Dice Tooltip
enhancedDiceTooltipMode.hint: Choose how NAS labels PF1 roll tooltip rows.

enhancedCombatText.name: Enhanced Combat Text
enhancedCombatText.hint: When enabled, NAS restyles PF1 health delta scrolling text so simultaneous damage, healing, and ability changes are easier to distinguish.

combatTextMode.name: Combat Text Mode
combatTextMode.hint: Choose how NAS presents PF1 health delta scrolling text.

cinematicCombatTextPreset.name: Cinematic Combat Text Preset
cinematicCombatTextPreset.hint: Choose the movement style NAS uses when Combat Text Mode is Cinematic.

skipSurprisedTokens.name: Skip Surprised Tokens
skipSurprisedTokens.hint: Enable to automatically skip combatants marked as Surprised during the surprise round.

enableWoundsVigorAutomation.name: Enable Wounds & Vigor Automation
enableWoundsVigorAutomation.hint: When enabled, NAS applies advanced Wounds & Vigor rules on top of PF1's base variant behavior.

woundsVigorActionTaxMode.name: Wounds & Vigor Action Tax Mode
woundsVigorActionTaxMode.hint: Choose which action types trigger the wounded action tax (-1 wound point and a DC 10 Constitution check).

choices.broad: Broad combat (move, standard, full, attack, AoO, round)

woundsVigorNoWoundsConstructUndead.name: W&V House Rule: Construct/Undead Use Vigor Only
woundsVigorNoWoundsConstructUndead.hint: When enabled, constructs and undead are treated as having no wound track for Wounds & Vigor automation, and are destroyed at 0 vigor.

woundsVigorWoundDamageTypeMenu.hint: Choose which damage types use Wounds & Vigor wound behavior when applied as wounds.

translationMenu.name: Configure Translations
translationMenu.label: Translations
translationMenu.hint: Set the translations for various terms for the module to recognize.

migrationTool.name: Run Legacy Migration
migrationTool.label: Open Migration Tool
migrationTool.hint: Copy legacy module data into this module's settings and flags.

reorderAllConditions.name: Reorder All Conditions Alphabetically
reorderAllConditions.hint: Toggle to reorder all conditions alphabetically or only new conditions added by this module.

automaticBuffs.name: Enable Automatic Buffs
automaticBuffs.hint: When enabled, the module will attempt to find and apply matching buffs when spells or consumables are used.

buffAutomationMode.name: Buff Automation Mode
buffAutomationMode.hint: Choose how strict the buff automation should be when no targets are selected.

buffTargetFiltering.name: Buff Target Filtering
buffTargetFiltering.hint: Choose how buff targets are filtered. "By Disposition" only applies buffs to targets with the same disposition as the caster, "All Targets" applies buffs to all selected targets, and "Manual Selection" prompts you to choose which targets receive the buff.

buffCompendiaSelector.label: Select Compendia
buffCompendiaSelector.hint: Choose which compendia to include when searching for buff items.

customBuffCompendia.name: Custom Buff Compendia
customBuffCompendia.hint: Select additional compendia containing buffs to include in the automated buff search.

automateConditions.name: Automate Conditions
automateConditions.hint: Master toggle for condition automation settings. When disabled, condition automation features in this section will not execute.

handleConfused.name: Automate Confused Condition Actions
handleConfused.hint: Enable to automatically generate a message at the start of each round to determine the actions of confused tokens.

restrictMovement.name: Restrict Movement
restrictMovement.hint: Choose who is restricted from moving when affected by immobilizing conditions such as 'anchored', 'cowering', 'dazed', 'dying', 'helpless', 'paralyzed', 'petrified', or 'pinned'. 'Players Only' allows GMs to always move tokens. 'Disabled' will allow all movement.

autoApplyFF.name: Auto Apply Flat-Footed Condition
autoApplyFF.hint: Enable to automatically apply the flat-footed condition to any token with an initiative roll result lower than the highest when combat begins.

blindMovementCheck.name: Enable Blind Movement Notification
blindMovementCheck.hint: Enable to notify users to roll an Acrobatics check when a blind token attempts to move.

disableAtZeroHP.name: Apply Disabled Condition at 0 HP
disableAtZeroHP.hint: Automatically apply the disabled condition based on the selected option.

autoApplyED.name: Auto Apply Energy Drain
autoApplyED.hint: Enable to automatically apply the energy drain condition to any token with negative levels.

entangledGrappledHandling.name: Concentration Check for Entangled and Grappled
entangledGrappledHandling.hint: Choose whether to prompt concentration checks for entangled and/or grappled spellcasting.

grappledHandling.name: Grappled Action Handling
grappledHandling.hint: Choose how actions requiring two hands should be handled when grappled: Strict, Lenient, or Disabled.

nauseatedHandling.name: Nauseated Action Handling
nauseatedHandling.hint: Choose how actions are handled when affected by the nauseated condition: Strict, Lenient, or Disabled.

squeezedHandling.name: Squeezed Action Handling
squeezedHandling.hint: Choose how actions are handled when affected by the squeezed condition: Strict, Lenient, or Disabled.

squeezedExitHandling.name: Squeezed Exit Handling
squeezedExitHandling.hint: Choose whether movement leaving a narrow area should still count for squeezing classification.

choices.ignore: Ignore squeezing when exiting a narrow area

automateSqueezing.name: Automate Squeezing
automateSqueezing.hint: Enable squeezing and squeezed automation. Additional squeezing settings are available in the configuration form.

squeezedEscapeFailureHandling.name: Squeezed Escape Failure Handling
squeezedEscapeFailureHandling.hint: Choose where movement stops when Escape Artist fails while entering a less-than-half-width area.

choices.enterFirstNarrowSquare: Enter the narrow area and stop at the first square

squeezingMediumBodyWidth.name: Squeezing Medium Body Width
squeezingMediumBodyWidth.hint: Overall body width baseline for Medium tokens (in grid squares). This value scales by token size.

squeezingMediumHeadWidth.name: Squeezing Medium Head Width
squeezingMediumHeadWidth.hint: Head width baseline for Medium tokens (in grid squares). Spaces narrower than this stop movement.

squeezingEscapeArtistDC.name: Squeezing Escape Artist DC
squeezingEscapeArtistDC.hint: Escape Artist DC used for less-than-half-width squeezing checks.

unconsciousAtNegativeHP.name: Apply Unconscious Condition at Negative HP
unconsciousAtNegativeHP.hint: Automatically apply the unconscious condition based on the selected option.

enableDyingAutomation.name: Enable Dying Automation
enableDyingAutomation.hint: Automate dying condition maintenance and start-of-turn stabilization checks (DC 10 Constitution check with negative HP penalty).

dyingHandling.name: Dying Action Handling
dyingHandling.hint: Choose how actions are handled while dying: Strict (block all), Lenient (allow with warning), or Disabled.

applyDeadCondition.name: Apply Dead Condition at Negative Constitution HP
applyDeadCondition.hint: Automatically apply the dead condition based on the selected option.{monksSuffix}
applyDeadCondition.monksSuffix: Enabling this option will disable the Monks Combat Details auto defeated setting.
applyDeadCondition.conflictContent: Enabling this setting will disable the auto-defeated setting of Monks Combat Details. Do you want to proceed?
applyDeadCondition.monksDisabledInfo: Monks Combat Details auto-defeated setting has been disabled.
applyDeadCondition.applyDeadDisabledWarning: Apply Dead Condition setting has been disabled.

removeDeadCondition.name: Auto Remove Dead Condition
removeDeadCondition.hint: Choose when to automatically remove the dead condition.

choices.aboveNegativeCon: When HP is above -CON (e.g., -12 with 13 CON)

modifierNameSettings.label: Customize Modifiers
modifierNameSettings.hint: Edit the display names for common buff/spell modifiers (e.g., Lesser, Greater, Mass, Communal, etc.)

variantMappingManager.hint: View and remove remembered variant assignments for spells/abilities.

modifierNames.name: Buff/Spell Modifier Names
modifierNames.hint: Stores the custom names for buff/spell modifiers.

communalHandling.name: Communal Spell Duration Handling
communalHandling.hint: Choose how communal spell durations are divided among targets: divide evenly (prompt if not possible), or always prompt the caster to divide.

personalTargeting.name: Personal Spell Targeting
personalTargeting.hint: Choose whether personal spells can target tokens other than the caster.

choices.allow: Allow targets other than the caster
choices.deny: Deny targets that are not the caster

variantTargetCap.name: Variant Target Cap Handling
variantTargetCap.hint: When a spell has a detected target cap (e.g., Blessing of Fervor), choose whether to enforce it (cancel if exceeded) or only show a warning hint.

pairedBuffMappings.name: Paired Buff Variant Mappings
pairedBuffMappings.hint: Internal store for per-spell variant to group assignments.

messages.componentsInfo: Add one or more damage rolls with their own formulas and damage types.

translationForm.description: Configure translated trait names and optional class/race aliases used for language-sensitive automation matching.

variantMappingManager.title: Manage Variant Mappings
variantMappingManager.notes: View and remove remembered variant assignments for spells and abilities. Check entries to remove and click Save.

buffCompendiaSelector.title: Select Buff Compendia
buffCompendiaSelector.notes: Select compendia that contain buff items to include in the automated buff search.

woundDamageTypesForm.title: W&V Wound Damage Types
woundDamageTypesForm.description: Checked damage types will use dice amount / caster level when applying direct wound damage.

squeezingAutomationConfig.title: Squeezing Automation Configuration
squeezingAutomationConfig.description: Configure squeezing and squeezed automation behavior.
squeezingAutomationConfig.sizeDescription: Size values are Medium baselines in grid squares and scale with token size.

tooltips.bodyWidth: Medium baseline body width. Leave empty to use module default.
tooltips.headWidth: Medium baseline head width. Leave empty to use module default.

confirmations.resetDefaults: Are you sure you want to reset to default values?

errors.imgOrIconRequired: Either an image or an icon class is required.

warnings.hardnessFlagsDeprecated: [{moduleId}] Hardness boolean/dictionary flags are deprecated and will not be supported in the future. Please update to use item options.

itemSettings.Title: Item Damage Settings
itemSettings.Description: Configure global damage automation settings for this item.

actionSettings.Description: Configure damage automation settings for this specific action.

settings.BypassHardnessHint: Ignores hardness entirely when dealing damage.
settings.IgnoreHardnessHint: Ignores a specific amount of hardness when dealing damage.
settings.BypassImmunityHint: Bypasses immunity to selected damage types.
settings.BypassResistanceHint: Bypasses resistance to selected damage types.
settings.BypassDRHint: Bypasses DR for selected damage types.

anchored.label: Anchored
anchored.description:
Fixed to a particular location.

Cannot be moved by any external force, including spells like telekinesis.

Cannot perform actions requiring movement.

Can still attack or cast spells if they do not involve changing position.

burning.label: Burning
burning.description: A creature that is burning takes 1d6 fire damage immediately and at the start of each subsequent round until the flames are extinguished. Each round, the creature may attempt a DC 15 Reflex save to put out the fire (success ends the condition). Jumping into water or smothering the flames grants a +4 bonus on this save. Flammable items may also catch fire and require their own saves.

concealed.label: Concealed
concealed.description: Miss chance applies to attacks because the target is concealed. Normal concealment has a lower success threshold than total concealment.

disabled.label: Disabled
disabled.description:
Character with 0 or stable negative hit points.

Can take a single move or standard action each round (but not both).

Moves at half speed.

Standard actions deal 1 damage after completion, risking negative hit points and dying.

Can take swift, immediate, and free actions.

Recovers hit points naturally if helped.

Otherwise, DC 10 Constitution check after 8 hours rest to start natural recovery.

Penalty on check equals negative hit point total.

Failure causes 1 hit point loss but does not cause unconsciousness.

dying.label: Dying
dying.description:
Unconscious and near death.

Negative hit points and not stabilized.

Can take no actions.

On next turn after being reduced to negative hit points, and all subsequent turns:

- DC 10 Constitution check to become stable.

- Penalty on roll equals negative hit point total.

- Natural 20 is an automatic success.

- Failure results in losing 1 hit point.

Stable characters do not need to make this check.

If negative hit points equal Constitution score, the character dies.

energyDrained.label: Energy Drained
energyDrained.description:
Gains one or more negative levels.

Each negative level:

- Cumulative -1 penalty on ability checks, attack rolls, CMB, CMD, saves, and skill checks.

- Reduces current and total HP by 5.

- Treated as one level lower for level-dependent variables (e.g., spellcasting).

If negative levels equal Hit Dice, the character dies.

Temporary energy drains:

- New save each day to remove (DC same as original effect).

Permanent energy drains:

- No daily save to remove.

- Can be removed by spells like restoration.

- Remain after revival.

- Must be removed for effective revival if they equal Hit Dice.

Energy drain is not a death effect.

Death ward grants immunity to energy drain and suspends penalties while active.

fascinated.label: Fascinated
fascinated.description:
Entranced by a supernatural or spell effect.

Stands or sits quietly, taking no actions other than paying attention to the effect.

-4 penalty on skill checks made as reactions (e.g., Perception checks).

Any potential threat (e.g., hostile creature approaching) allows a new saving throw.

Any obvious threat (e.g., drawing a weapon, casting a spell, aiming a ranged weapon) automatically breaks the effect.

An ally can shake the fascinated creature free as a standard action.

grappled.label: Grappled
grappled.description:
Cannot move and is treated as immobilized.

Casting or using spell-like abilities requires a concentration check (DC 10 + spell level + grappler's latest grapple check result).

grappling.label: Grappling
grappling.description:
You are controlling a grapple and are slowed.

Targets you successfully grapple gain the grappled condition.

squeezed.label: Squeezed
squeezed.description:
You are squeezing through a space narrower than half your width.

You take a -4 penalty to AC and attack rolls, lose your Dexterity bonus to AC, and cannot attack while in this state.

flatFooted.label: Flat-Footed
flatFooted.description:
Has not yet acted during combat.

Loses Dexterity bonus to AC and CMD.

Cannot make attacks of opportunity, unless having Combat Reflexes feat or Uncanny Dodge class ability.

Characters with Uncanny Dodge retain Dexterity bonus to AC and can make attacks of opportunity before acting in the first round.

Cannot take immediate actions while flat-footed.

immobilized.label: Immobilized
immobilized.description:
Unable to move from current location.

Cannot take any move actions.

Cannot reposition itself.

Can still perform actions that do not require movement (e.g., attacking, casting spells).

petrified.label: Petrified
petrified.description:
Turned to stone and considered unconscious.

If cracked or broken, but pieces are joined with the body upon returning to flesh, the character is unharmed.

If the petrified body is incomplete when returning to flesh, the body remains incomplete, resulting in permanent hit point loss and/or debilitation.

slowed.label: Slowed
slowed.description:
Movement is significantly hindered.

Movement speed is typically reduced by half.

May take fewer actions per turn, often limited to one standard action or move action.

stable.label: Stable
stable.description:
No longer dying but still unconscious.

Stopped losing hit points each round while still having negative hit points.

If stabilized by aid (e.g., Heal check or magical healing), no longer loses hit points.

Can make a DC 10 Constitution check each hour to become conscious and disabled (penalty equals negative hit point total).

If stabilized without aid, still at risk of losing hit points.

Can make a Constitution check each hour to become stable (as if received aid), but each failed check causes 1 hit point loss.

unconscious.label: Unconscious
unconscious.description:
Knocked out and helpless.

Can result from having negative hit points (but not more than the creature's Constitution score) or from nonlethal damage exceeding current hit points.

effects.3a: draws their {itemName} and inflicts {damage} points of damage to themselves with it.
effects.3b: strikes themselves in the face with their fists, causing {damage} points of bludgeoning damage.

main.GrappledTwoHands: {name} cannot perform this action due to being grappled and it requires two hands.
main.GrappledLenient: {name} is grappled but can perform the action under lenient handling.
main.NauseatedStrict: {name} cannot perform this action due to being nauseated; only move actions are allowed.
main.NauseatedLenient: {name} is nauseated but can perform other actions under lenient handling.
main.SqueezedStrict: {name} cannot perform attack actions due to being squeezed.
main.SqueezedLenient: {name} is squeezed but can perform attacks under lenient handling.
main.DyingLenient: {name} is dying but can perform actions under lenient handling.
main.GrappleNoTargets: Grapple requires at least one target.
main.GrappleFootnote: +[[5]] circumstance bonus on grapple checks made against the same grappled target in subsequent rounds.
main.GrappleConcentrationDC: Concentration DC vs grapple: {dc} (10 + spell level {spellLevel} + grapple check {grappleTotal}).
main.WoundsVigorConCheckPassed: {name} acts while wounded: Constitution check {total} vs DC {dc} succeeded.
main.WoundsVigorConCheckFailed: {name} acts while wounded: Constitution check {total} vs DC {dc} failed and they fall unconscious.
